title: node-postgres Pool, Client Ownership, Transactions, and Shutdown · Inference Brief: Feed Ts Row Runtime Validation
summary: Each request creates a pool, transaction statements call pool.query, checked-out clients leak on error, row values are trusted, and process exit races pool timers. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed ts row runtime validation increment in bounded pg repository and transaction port. Learners progress through…
objectives: Treat query rows as external values, request explicit columns, validate scalar and null states, and reject cardinality or schema drift.
Run BEGIN, every statement, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on the same checked-out client and prohibit pool.query inside the unit of work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
